---
title: "Lecture 08 Notes: Robot Simulation - Modern Physics Engines and Contact Dynamics"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 08 slides](../slides/08-robot-simulation.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Simulation enables fast prototyping and reproducible testing.
- Physics engines approximate contact, friction, and constraints numerically.
- Stable simulation requires consistent integration step and model parameters.

## Practical checklist

1. Validate inertial and contact parameters.
2. Use fixed time steps for controller benchmarking.
3. Compare simulated trajectories against hardware logs.

## Review questions

1. Why do contact-rich tasks diverge more in sim-to-real transfer?
2. How does step size affect stability and runtime?
3. What metrics best indicate simulation fidelity for your task?
